text.title: prototyping Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for prototyping as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Too high fidelity too early — polishing visuals before flow validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wastes design time and creates emotional attachment to visuals that testing may invalidate.

failure-mode.description: Building a prototype but not testing it with users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The artifact has no value without learning; testing is the point.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring test findings — iterating the design without addressing discovered issues.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Testing without acting on results is a waste of participant time and produces no product improvement.

failure-mode.description: No clear hypothesis — prototyping without defined questions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a hypothesis, findings cannot be evaluated and the next iteration has no direction.

failure-mode.description: Leading questions in test script ("Was that easy?", "Did you find it intuitive?").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Suggestive phrasing skews answers toward positive bias; neutral phrasing ("describe what you just did") produces valid data.
